title: Pre-Release Quality Sprint

credentials.note: Temporary credentials for this sprint run. Will be changed after completion.

environment.note: ALWAYS activate venv before running pytest, ruff, or python commands. Tools also available at ~/.local/bin/ if venv activation fails. Run: source venv/bin/activate && export PYTHONPATH=. before any Python work.

parallel_warning: Multiple agents may run in parallel on different tracks (frontend vs backend). If you are running in parallel with another agent: (1) Do NOT touch files outside your assigned scope. (2) When writing to chain_log.json, read the file fresh first to avoid overwriting the other agent's updates. (3) If you get a git merge conflict on chain_log.json, re-read the file and re-apply only YOUR session entry. (4) Use git pull before committing to pick up the other agent's commits.

sessions.title: Comprehensive Audit: Establish Baseline Score
tasks_completed: Ran all 7 automated checks (ruff, eslint, build, boundary, frontend tests, backend tests, startup import)
Launched 5 parallel audit subagents (backend source, API/security, test suite, frontend hygiene, conventions)
Scored all 10 dimensions of the quality rubric
Compiled 95 findings with file:line, severity, and target session assignments
Wrote audit_baseline.md to prompts/0950_chain/
deviations: Ruff 0.15.9 installed fresh (not in venv) — reported 11 EXE rules not in baseline version. Excluded from core comparison but noted.
Subagent 3 (test suite) ran long; test failure analysis supplemented by direct investigation
blockers_encountered: Ruff and pytest not pre-installed in venv — installed during session
PYTHONPATH needed for startup import check and pytest
sessions.notes_for_next: Overall score dropped to 7.9/10 from 8.5 baseline. Primary drivers: 94 test failures (83 FE + 11 BE), ESLint 17 warnings (budget 8), and accumulated frontend hygiene debt. 0950b should prioritize the 3 SECURITY findings first. The AgentExecution CheckConstraint mismatch (idle/sleeping not in constraint) is the highest-risk item — verify whether the migration actually enforces the constraint before writing a fix. Test failures (0950l) are the biggest single score impact but are downstream in the chain.
cascading_impacts: Frontend test failure count (83) is higher than expected — 0950l may need more time than originally estimated
ESLint warnings (17) are well over budget — 0950c must remove at least 9 warnings to meet budget of 8
sessions.summary: Baseline audit complete. Score: 7.9/10 (down from 8.5). 95 findings across 4 severity levels: 3 SECURITY, 8 HIGH, 62 MEDIUM, 22 LOW. Output: prompts/0950_chain/audit_baseline.md

sessions.title: Backend: Security + Dict-Returns + Exception Annotations
tasks_completed: SECURITY: Added Depends(get_current_active_user) to /health/database endpoint (configuration.py)
SECURITY: Added idle+sleeping to AgentExecution CheckConstraint + incremental migration (agent_identity.py, baseline_v35)
SECURITY: CORS wildcard entries now filtered out instead of just warned (app.py)
HIGH: Fixed redundant except (ImportError, Exception) → except Exception with BLE001 noqa (database.py)
HIGH: Annotated broad catch in tool_accessor.py:574
MEDIUM: Converted 4 dict-return endpoints to Pydantic response models (lifecycle.py, configuration.py x2, messages.py)
MEDIUM: Resolved lifecycle.py:68 TODO — ProductService handles project deactivation internally (Option B)
MEDIUM: Refactored downloads.py bootstrap-prompt and generate-token to use Depends(get_current_active_user)
LOW: Fixed file permissions (chmod -x on 10 __init__.py files, chmod +x on run_api.py)
LOW: Deleted stale # REMOVED: comment in users.py:26
LOW: Annotated 3 broad catches in downloads/content_generator.py
LOW: Added [tool.ruff] config section to pyproject.toml
deviations: Renamed test_database_connection → check_database_health to avoid PT028 ruff false positive (function name starts with test_)
Used venv instead of system Python for validation — system packages lack project dependencies
sessions.notes_for_next: All 13 0950b findings resolved. Zero new test failures introduced — the 11 pre-existing failures in test_project_service_helpers.py remain (assigned to 0950l). Ruff passes clean. CORS wildcards are now actively rejected. The DB health endpoint now requires auth. The incremental migration 0950b_exec_status must be applied to running databases with: alembic upgrade head. 0950f (next in backend chain) can proceed — dead code removal is unblocked.
cascading_impacts: The /health/database endpoint now requires authentication — any monitoring scripts calling it unauthenticated will need updating
CORS wildcard rejection means config.yaml entries with * are silently stripped — document this if needed
sessions.orchestrator_directives: PRIORITY ORDER from 0950a audit: (1) SECURITY: Add auth to DB health endpoint config.py:691, fix AgentExecution CheckConstraint agent_identity.py:302 to include idle/sleeping, reject CORS wildcards app.py:298-301. (2) HIGH: Fix redundant except in database.py:93, annotate broad catch tool_accessor.py:574. (3) MEDIUM: Fix 4 dict-return endpoints (lifecycle.py:185, configuration.py:175+707, messages.py:272), fix file permissions (chmod -x on 10 files, chmod +x run_api.py), resolve TODO lifecycle.py:68, fix auth pattern in downloads.py:455-575. Read prompts/0950_chain/audit_baseline.md for full details. Use PYTHONPATH=. for pytest and startup check. Ruff needs installing: pip install ruff. sudo password and postgres password: 4010.
sessions.summary: All 13 assigned findings resolved: 3 SECURITY (auth on DB health, CheckConstraint +idle/sleeping, CORS wildcard rejection), 2 HIGH (redundant except, broad catch annotation), 6 MEDIUM (4 dict-returns to Pydantic models, lifecycle TODO, downloads auth pattern), 4 LOW (file permissions, stale comment, 3 broad catch annotations, ruff config). Zero new test failures. Ruff clean. Startup OK.

sessions.title: Frontend: Hardcoded Hex Color Sweep
tasks_completed: DEAD SCSS: Deleted 3 dead mixins from agent-colors.scss (agent-card-border, chat-head-badge, status-badge) — zero @include usages

TOKENS: Added 15 new SCSS variables to design-tokens.scss ($color-accent-success, $color-accent-danger, $color-accent-special, $color-status-idle/sleeping/decommissioned/staged, $color-brand-yellow-hover, $color-text-on-muted, $color-text-hover, $color-pill-border, $color-indicator-disconnected, $color-card-git/serena, $color-phase-amber)

HEX SWEEP: Replaced hardcoded hex in 12 core files (findings #19-30): RecentProjectsList, StatusBadge, RoleBadge, MessagePanel, NavigationDrawer, WelcomeView, SetupStep2Connect, statusConfig.js, SystemSettings, UserSettings, AiToolConfigWizard, BroadcastPanel

HEX SWEEP: Extended sweep to 15 additional files: MessagesView, DashboardView, ProductsView, TasksView, ProjectsView, AgentTableView, GitIntegrationCard, McpIntegrationCard, SerenaIntegrationCard, AgentExport, ProjectTabs, ProductDetailsDialog, ProductTuningReview, JobsTab, MessageItem

PATTERN: <style> blocks use SCSS variables ($color-*) or CSS custom properties (var(--agent-*, --text-muted, --color-accent-*)). Template inline styles use CSS vars. JS scripts use named constants with // $token-name comments tracing to design-tokens.scss
CLEANUP: Replaced all var(--brand-yellow, #ffc300) fallbacks with var(--color-accent-primary) since --brand-yellow was never defined
CLEANUP: Removed all design-token-exempt comments (not an official exemption category)
CLEANUP: Replaced local hexToRgb in StatusBadge with hexToRgba from @/utils/colorUtils. Replaced local tintedBg in WelcomeView with hexToRgba
deviations: statusConfig.js treated as a JS token definition file (like agentColors.js) — hex values defined in named STATUS_COLORS constant map at top of file, not imported from external source
theme.js and constants.js not modified — they are Vuetify theme config and swatch palette definitions respectively, functionally token definition files
rgba() background values in template inline styles (e.g. rgba(103,189,109,0.15)) partially remain where no CSS variable equivalent exists — the color: values were all tokenized
Vuetify color prop hex values (e.g. color='#ffffff') kept as named constants with // $token-name comments since Vuetify props don't support CSS variables
sessions.notes_for_next: 0950k: Large components with complex style sections: WelcomeView.vue (960+ lines, 28 card definitions with agent colors), JobsTab.vue (1500+ lines style block with nested selectors), ProductsView.vue (1200+ lines), ProjectTabs.vue (900+ lines). These are candidates for component splits. The following files were edited by 0950e and should be handled carefully: StatusBadge.vue (now imports getAgentColor + hexToRgba), WelcomeView.vue (now imports hexToRgba), ProductTuningReview.vue (now imports getAgentColor + hexToRgba), GitIntegrationCard.vue (now imports hexToRgba), SerenaIntegrationCard.vue (now imports hexToRgba). WelcomeView.vue card definitions now use getAgentColor() imports — card accent colors are dynamic, not hardcoded

sessions.title: God-Class Split: ThinClientPromptGenerator
tasks_completed: Created src/giljo_mcp/prompts/ subpackage with __init__.py re-exports

Extracted StagingPromptBuilder (staging_prompt_builder.py, 224 lines) — _generate_thin_prompt, _regenerate_mission, generate_staging_prompt build logic
Deleted 4 dead methods (~250 lines): _build_thin_prompt, _build_thin_prompt_with_memory, _inject_360_memory, _inject_git_instructions — all were unreachable (only called by each other)
Refactored generate() from 271 lines to 91 lines by extracting _resolve_user_config (52 lines) and _find_or_create_orchestrator (104 lines)
ThinClientPromptGenerator reduced from 1676 lines to 691 lines — dispatcher pattern, public interface unchanged
Updated test_0830 to call generate_implementation_prompt() instead of private _build_multi_terminal_orchestrator_prompt()

Gemini and multi-terminal prompts placed in CodexPromptBuilder (not a separate file) since they follow the same pattern. Handover listed 3 builder files; Gemini+multi-terminal are small enough to co-locate with Codex.
Deleted 4 dead methods not explicitly listed in handover scope — required to meet 800-line ceiling and discovered during Phase 1 mapping.
